info.title: AgentMail API
info.description: AgentMail email API endpoints exposed through the AIsa unified gateway. Adapted from the upstream docs.agentmail.to OpenAPI spec.

human_email.description: Email address of the human who owns the agent. A 6-digit OTP will be sent to this address.

username.description: Username for the auto-created inbox (e.g. "my-agent" creates my-agent@agentmail.to).

source.description:
The SDK, framework, or platform issuing this sign-up (e.g. `agentmail-python`, `agentmail-cli`, `agentmail-mcp`).
Identifies the caller — answers "who is signing up".
Max 2048 characters.

referrer.description:
The channel that drove this sign-up — where the agent or its developer discovered AgentMail
(e.g. `agent.email`, a partner URL, a campaign tag). Answers "where did this sign-up come from".
Max 2048 characters.

type_agent_AgentSignupRequest.description: Request body to sign up an agent.
type_agent_AgentSignupRequest.title: AgentSignupRequest

api_key.description: API key for authenticating subsequent requests. Store this securely, it cannot be retrieved again.

name.description: Machine-readable error name identifying the validation failure.

errors.description: Details of the validation problems with the request.

otp_code.description: 6-digit verification code sent to the human's email address.

type_agent_AgentVerifyRequest.description: Request body to verify an agent with an OTP code.
type_agent_AgentVerifyRequest.title: AgentVerifyRequest

type__Limit.description: Limit of number of items returned.
type__Limit.title: Limit

type_inboxes_MetadataValue.description: A metadata value. May be a string, number, or boolean.
type_inboxes_MetadataValue.title: MetadataValue

type_inboxes_Metadata.description:
Custom key-value pairs attached to the inbox. Up to 256 keys. Keys and
string values are each limited to 256 characters. When updating metadata,
send a key with a null value to remove that key.
type_inboxes_Metadata.title: Metadata

pod_id.description: Identifier of the pod the resource belongs to.

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox the created resource belongs to.

email.description: Full email address of the created inbox.

display_name.description: Display name of the created inbox.

client_id.description: Caller-supplied identifier echoed back on the created resource.

metadata.description: Custom metadata attached to the created inbox.

updated_at.description: Time the draft was last updated.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

limit.description: The page-size limit that was applied to this response.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page; pass it back as `page_token`. Absent or null when there are no more pages.

inboxes.description: The page of inboxes, ordered by `created_at` descending.

name.description: Machine-readable error name identifying the failure.

username.description: Local part of the new inbox's address; a random value is generated when omitted.

domain.description: Domain for the new inbox's address; must be a verified domain, otherwise the platform default is used.

display_name.description: Display name shown on mail sent from the new inbox.

client_id.description: Caller-supplied identifier for idempotency and your own correlation of the created resource.

metadata.description: Custom key/value metadata to attach to the new inbox.

type_inboxes_UpdateMetadata.description:
Custom key-value pairs to merge into the inbox's existing metadata. A
value may be a string, number, boolean, or null. Setting a key to null
removes it. Up to 256 keys; keys and string values are each limited to
256 characters.
type_inboxes_UpdateMetadata.title: UpdateMetadata

display_name.description: New display name shown on outgoing mail from the inbox.

metadata.description: Key/value pairs merged into the inbox's existing metadata; include only the keys you want to change.

type__Before.description: Timestamp before which to filter by.
type__Before.title: Before

type__After.description: Timestamp after which to filter by.
type__After.title: After

type_threads_ThreadTimestamp.description: Timestamp of last sent or received message.
type_threads_ThreadTimestamp.title: ThreadTimestamp

type_threads_ThreadSenders.description: Senders in thread. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_threads_ThreadSenders.title: ThreadSenders

type_threads_ThreadRecipients.description: Recipients in thread. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_threads_ThreadRecipients.title: ThreadRecipients

type_threads_ThreadPreview.description: Text preview of last message in thread.
type_threads_ThreadPreview.title: ThreadPreview

attachment_id.description: Identifier of the attachment; pass it to get-attachment to obtain a download URL.

size.description: Size of the attachment in bytes.

content_type.description: MIME content type of the attachment (e.g. application/pdf).

content_disposition.description: How the attachment is meant to be presented: `inline` renders within the message body, `attachment` is a downloadable file.

content_id.description: Content-ID used to reference an inline attachment from the HTML body (cid: links).

type_threads_ThreadLastMessageId.description: ID of last message in thread.
type_threads_ThreadLastMessageId.title: ThreadLastMessageId

type_threads_ThreadUpdatedAt.description: Time at which thread was last updated.
type_threads_ThreadUpdatedAt.title: ThreadUpdatedAt

type_threads_ThreadCreatedAt.description: Time at which thread was created.
type_threads_ThreadCreatedAt.title: ThreadCreatedAt

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox that contains the thread.

timestamp.description: Time of the latest activity in the thread.

received_timestamp.description: Time the thread last received an inbound message.

sent_timestamp.description: Time the thread last sent an outbound message.

senders.description: Distinct sender addresses in the thread.

recipients.description: Distinct recipient addresses in the thread.

preview.description: Short plain-text snippet of the latest message in the thread.

last_message_id.description: Identifier of the most recent message in the thread.

message_count.description: Number of messages in the thread.

size.description: Total size of the thread in bytes.

updated_at.description: Time the thread was last updated.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

limit.description: The page-size limit that was applied to this response.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page; pass it back as `page_token`. Absent or null when there are no more pages.

threads.description: The page of threads, ordered by `timestamp` descending.

type_messages_MessageTimestamp.description: Time at which message was sent or drafted.
type_messages_MessageTimestamp.title: MessageTimestamp

type_messages_MessageFrom.description: Address of sender. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_messages_MessageFrom.title: MessageFrom

type_messages_MessageTo.description: Addresses of recipients. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_messages_MessageTo.title: MessageTo

type_messages_MessageCc.description: Addresses of CC recipients. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_messages_MessageCc.title: MessageCc

type_messages_MessageBcc.description: Addresses of BCC recipients. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_messages_MessageBcc.title: MessageBcc

type_messages_MessageInReplyTo.description: ID of message being replied to.
type_messages_MessageInReplyTo.title: MessageInReplyTo

type_messages_MessageReferences.description: IDs of previous messages in thread.
type_messages_MessageReferences.title: MessageReferences

type_messages_MessageUpdatedAt.description: Time at which message was last updated.
type_messages_MessageUpdatedAt.title: MessageUpdatedAt

type_messages_MessageCreatedAt.description: Time at which message was created.
type_messages_MessageCreatedAt.title: MessageCreatedAt

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox that contains this thread.

thread_id.description: Unique identifier of the thread, used to fetch, update or delete it.

labels.description: Labels currently applied to the thread, including system labels and any custom labels.

timestamp.description: Time of the most recent activity (last sent or received message) in the thread; use it to sort threads by recency.

reply_to.description: Reply-To addresses recipients should reply to instead of the From address.

bcc.description: BCC (blind carbon copy) recipient addresses, not visible to other recipients.

subject.description: Subject line of the thread, typically taken from its first message.

preview.description: Short plain-text snippet of the latest message, for showing a thread summary without loading full bodies.

extracted_text.description: Just the newly written plain text, with quoted/replied history stripped out.

extracted_html.description: Just the newly written HTML, with quoted/replied history stripped out.

attachments.description: All attachments accumulated across the thread's messages.

in_reply_to.description: ID of the message this draft/thread is replying to, if any.

references.description: IDs of earlier messages in the thread, forming the reply chain.

headers.description: Raw email headers of the message as key/value pairs.

size.description: Total size of the thread in bytes.

updated_at.description: Time the thread record was last updated.

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox that contains this thread.

thread_id.description: Unique identifier of the thread, used to fetch, update or delete it.

labels.description: Labels currently applied to the thread, including system labels and any custom labels.

timestamp.description: Time of the most recent activity (last sent or received message) in the thread; use it to sort threads by recency.

received_timestamp.description: Time the thread last received an inbound message; null if it has never received one.

sent_timestamp.description: Time the thread last sent an outbound message; null if nothing has been sent.

senders.description: Distinct sender addresses that have appeared in the thread.

recipients.description: Distinct recipient addresses that have appeared in the thread.

subject.description: Subject line of the thread, typically taken from its first message.

preview.description: Short plain-text snippet of the latest message, for showing a thread summary without loading full bodies.

attachments.description: All attachments accumulated across the thread's messages.

last_message_id.description: Identifier of the most recent message in the thread.

message_count.description: Number of messages currently in the thread.

size.description: Total size of the thread in bytes.

updated_at.description: Time the thread record was last updated.

messages.description: Full messages in the thread, ordered oldest-first by timestamp.

size.description: Size of the attachment in bytes.

content_type.description: MIME content type of the attachment.

content_disposition.description: `inline` if the attachment is meant to render within the body, `attachment` if it is a standalone download.

content_id.description: Content-ID for referencing an inline attachment from HTML.

download_url.description: Presigned URL to download the attachment bytes; short-lived, expiring at `expires_at`.

expires_at.description: Time after which the download URL stops working.

add_labels.description: Labels to attach. For threads, system labels are not allowed.

remove_labels.description: Labels to detach. For threads, system labels are not allowed and removal takes priority over additions of the same label.

labels.description: The message's labels after the update is applied.

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the message.

thread_id.description: Identifier of the thread the message belongs to.

timestamp.description: Time the message was sent or drafted.

to.description: Primary recipient addresses of the message.

preview.description: Short plain-text snippet of the message body.

in_reply_to.description: Identifier of the message this one replies to, if any.

references.description: Identifiers of earlier messages in the thread, forming the reply chain.

headers.description: Raw email headers of the message as key/value pairs.

size.description: Size of the message in bytes.

updated_at.description: Time the message was last updated.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

limit.description: The page-size limit that was applied to this response.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page; pass it back as `page_token`. Absent or null when there are no more pages.

messages.description: The page of messages, ordered by `timestamp` descending.

type_messages_BatchGetMessagesMessageIds.description:
IDs of messages to fetch. Maximum 500 ids per request. Duplicates are
rejected with a validation error. IDs not found in the inbox (including
cross-inbox or permission-restricted) are silently omitted from the
response; callers detect misses by comparing `count` against `limit`.
type_messages_BatchGetMessagesMessageIds.title: BatchGetMessagesMessageIds

messages.description:
Found messages. Order matches `message_ids` in the request. Body
fields (`text`, `html`, `extracted_text`, `extracted_html`) are
never populated; use the single-message endpoint to retrieve bodies.

message_id.description: Identifier of the message the raw source belongs to.

size.description: Size of the raw message in bytes.

download_url.description: Presigned URL to download the raw RFC 822 source from object storage; it is short-lived and expires at `expires_at`.

expires_at.description: Time after which the download URL stops working.

type_messages_RawMessageResponse.description: S3 presigned URL to download the raw .eml file.
type_messages_RawMessageResponse.title: RawMessageResponse

add_labels.description: Labels to attach. For threads, system labels are not allowed.

remove_labels.description: Labels to detach. For threads, system labels are not allowed and removal takes priority over additions of the same label.

labels.description: The message's labels after the update is applied.

content_type.description: MIME content type of the attachment.

content_disposition.description: `inline` to render within the body, `attachment` to offer as a download.

content_id.description: Content-ID used to reference an inline attachment from the HTML body.

content.description: The attachment bytes, base64-encoded. Use this or `url`, not both.

url.description: A URL the service fetches the attachment from. Use this or `content`, not both.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the forwarded message.

reply_to.description: Reply-to addresses for the forwarded message.

to.description: Recipients to forward the message to.

bcc.description: Blind carbon-copy recipients of the forward.

subject.description: Subject line of the forwarded message.

text.description: Additional plain-text body to prepend to the forward.

html.description: Additional HTML body to prepend to the forward.

attachments.description: Extra files to attach to the forward; provide each as base64 `content` or a fetchable `url`.

headers.description: Additional raw email headers to set on the forwarded message.

message_id.description: Identifier of the reply message that was sent.

thread_id.description: Identifier of the thread the reply was added to.

type_messages_ReplyAll.description: Reply to all recipients of the original message.
type_messages_ReplyAll.title: ReplyAll

labels.description: Labels to apply to the reply message.

reply_to.description: Reply-to addresses for the reply message.

to.description: Override the recipient addresses for the reply, instead of deriving them from the original message.

cc.description: Override the carbon-copy addresses for the reply.

bcc.description: Override the blind carbon-copy addresses for the reply.

reply_all.description: When true, address the reply to all original recipients (To/Cc) instead of only the original sender.

attachments.description: Files to attach to the reply; provide each as base64 `content` or a fetchable `url`.

headers.description: Additional raw email headers to set on the reply.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the reply-all message.

reply_to.description: Reply-to addresses for the reply-all message.

attachments.description: Files to attach; provide each as base64 `content` or a fetchable `url`.

headers.description: Additional raw email headers to set on the reply-all message.

type_drafts_DraftCc.description: Addresses of CC recipients. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.

type_drafts_DraftBcc.description: Addresses of BCC recipients. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.

send_at.description: Scheduled send time of the draft.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

limit.description: The page-size limit that was applied to this response.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page; pass it back as `page_token`. Absent or null when there are no more pages.

drafts.description: The page of drafts, ordered by `updated_at` descending.

type_drafts_DraftReplyTo.description: Reply-to addresses. In format `username@domain.com` or `Display Name <username@domain.com>`.
type_drafts_DraftReplyTo.title: DraftReplyTo

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox that contains this thread.

draft_id.description: ID of the draft, usable as the path parameter for draft operations.

client_id.description: Caller-supplied client ID echoed back, useful for correlating the draft with your own records.

labels.description: Labels currently applied to the thread, including system labels and any custom labels.

reply_to.description: Reply-To addresses recipients should reply to instead of the From address.

bcc.description: BCC (blind carbon copy) recipient addresses, not visible to other recipients.

subject.description: Subject line of the thread, typically taken from its first message.

preview.description: Short plain-text snippet of the latest message, for showing a thread summary without loading full bodies.

attachments.description: All attachments accumulated across the thread's messages.

in_reply_to.description: ID of the message this draft/thread is replying to, if any.

references.description: IDs of earlier messages in the thread, forming the reply chain.

send_status.description: Scheduled-send state of the draft: `scheduled` (queued for `send_at`), `sending`, or `failed`. Absent for drafts with no scheduled send.

send_at.description: When the draft is scheduled to be sent.

updated_at.description: Time the thread record was last updated.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the new draft.

reply_to.description: Reply-to addresses for the draft, when replies should go somewhere other than the sender.

to.description: Primary recipient addresses for the draft.

cc.description: Carbon-copy recipient addresses for the draft.

bcc.description: Blind carbon-copy recipient addresses for the draft.

attachments.description: Files to attach to the draft; provide each either inline as base64 `content` or as a fetchable `url`.

in_reply_to.description: Identifier of a message this draft replies to, to keep it in the same thread.

send_at.description: Time to schedule the draft for delayed sending.

client_id.description: Caller-supplied identifier for idempotency and your own correlation of the created resource.

reply_to.description: Replacement reply-to addresses for the draft.

to.description: Replacement primary recipient addresses for the draft.

cc.description: Replacement carbon-copy addresses for the draft.

bcc.description: Replacement blind carbon-copy addresses for the draft.

subject.description: New subject line for the draft.

text.description: New plain-text body for the draft.

html.description: New HTML body for the draft.

send_at.description: New scheduled send time for the draft; set it to schedule delayed delivery.

entry.description: The email address or domain to add to the allow/block list.

reason.description: Free-text reason recorded with the list entry.

type_metrics_MetricEventTypes.title: MetricEventTypes

type_metrics_Period.description: Period in number of seconds for the query.

type_metrics_MetricLimit.title: MetricLimit

count.description: Count of events in the bucket.

type_inbox-events_InboxEventType.description:
Type of inbox event. Wire format is dot.case to match the
convention used by webhook events (`message.received`,
`domain.verified`, etc. in events.yml). Pre-2026-04 these were
`label_added`/`label_removed` (snake_case). The Fern enum's `name`
field stays uppercase-snake (Fern convention); only the wire
`value` changed.
type_inbox-events_InboxEventType.title: InboxEventType

organization_id.description: Identifier of the organization that owns the event.

pod_id.description: Identifier of the pod the event belongs to.

inbox_id.description: Identifier of the inbox the event occurred in.

event_id.description: Unique identifier of the event; also drives the descending sort order.

event_type.description: What happened: `label.added` when a label was attached, `label.removed` when one was detached.

message_id.description: Identifier of the message the event relates to.

label.description: The label that was added or removed.

created_at.description: Time the event record was written.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

limit.description: The page-size limit that was applied to this response.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page; pass it back as `page_token`. Absent or null when there are no more pages.

events.description: The page of inbox events, ordered by `event_id` descending.

type_api-keys_ApiKeyPermissions.description: Granular permissions for the API key. When ommitted all permissions are granted. Otherwise, only permissions set to true are granted.
type_api-keys_ApiKeyPermissions.title: ApiKeyPermissions

type_api-keys_CreatedAt.description: Time at which api key was created.
type_api-keys_CreatedAt.title: CreatedAt

pod_id.description: Pod ID the api key is scoped to. If set, the key can only access resources within this pod.

inbox_id.description: Inbox ID the api key is scoped to. If set, the key can only access resources within this inbox.

used_at.description: Time at which api key was last used.

pod_id.description: Pod ID the api key is scoped to.

inbox_id.description: Inbox ID the api key is scoped to.

type_events_EventTypes.description: Event types for which to send events.
type_events_EventTypes.title: EventTypes

type_events_PodIds.description: Pods for which to send events. Maximum 10 per webhook.
type_events_PodIds.title: PodIds

type_events_InboxIds.description: Inboxes for which to send events. Maximum 10 per webhook.
type_events_InboxIds.title: InboxIds

updated_at.description: Time at which webhook was last updated.

created_at.description: Time at which webhook was created.

event_types.description:
Full list of event types this webhook should receive. At least one type is required. Send every type you
want in this array (not incremental). See [Webhooks overview](https://docs.agentmail.to/webhooks-overview)
for spam, blocked, and unauthenticated events and required permissions.

add_inbox_ids.description: Inbox IDs to subscribe to the webhook.

remove_inbox_ids.description: Inbox IDs to unsubscribe from the webhook.

add_pod_ids.description: Pod IDs to subscribe to the webhook.

remove_pod_ids.description: Pod IDs to unsubscribe from the webhook.

event_types.description:
When you send a non-empty list, it replaces the webhook's subscribed event types in full (the same
"set the list" behavior as create). It is not a merge or diff: include every event type you want after
the update. Sending a one-element array means the webhook will only receive that one type afterward.
Omit this field or send an empty array to leave event types unchanged. Clearing all types with an empty
list is not supported. Subscribing to `message.received.spam`, `message.received.blocked`, or
`message.received.unauthenticated` requires the matching label permission on the API key.

type_domains_FeedbackEnabled.description: Bounce and complaint notifications are sent to your inboxes.
type_domains_FeedbackEnabled.title: FeedbackEnabled

updated_at.description: Time at which the domain was last updated.

created_at.description: Time at which the domain was created.

type_domains_Status.description: The verification status of the domain.
type_domains_Status.title: Status

type.description: The type of the DNS record.

name.description: The name or host of the record.

status.description: The verification status of this specific record.

priority.description: The priority of the MX record.

records.description: A list of DNS records required to verify the domain.

updated_at.description: Time at which the domain was last updated.

created_at.description: Time at which the domain was created.

entry.description: The email address or domain of the list entry.

organization_id.description: ID of the organization the list entry belongs to.

reason.description: Free-text reason recorded when the entry was added.

direction.description: Traffic direction the entry governs: `send`, `receive`, or `reply`.

list_type.description: Whether this is an `allow` or `block` list entry.

entry_type.description: Whether the entry matches a single `email` address or an entire `domain`.

read_only.description: When true, the entry is system-managed and cannot be deleted through the API.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

limit.description: The page-size limit that was applied to this response.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page; pass it back as `page_token`. Absent or null when there are no more pages.

entries.description: The page of list entries, ordered by entry value ascending.

updated_at.description: Time at which pod was last updated.

created_at.description: Time at which pod was created.

updated_at.description: Time at which organization was last updated.

created_at.description: Time at which organization was created.

type_organizations_Organization.description: Organization details with usage limits and counts.
type_organizations_Organization.title: Organization

type_events_MessageReceivedEvent.description: A message was received. Spam, blocked, and unauthenticated received-message events use the same payload shape with different `event_type` values.
type_events_MessageReceivedEvent.title: MessageReceivedEvent

get.description: Retrieve a single AgentMail resource by its path identifiers. The exact resource returned depends on the route: an inbox, a draft, a message, a thread, or a single allow/block list entry. The full object (including bodies, attachments and timestamps) is returned, so this is the canonical way to fetch the complete state of one item after listing.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the thread to retrieve. Returned by the threads list and present on every message.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox. Send as `Bearer <api_key>`; keep it secret and never log it in clear text.

patch.description: Partially update an existing resource identified by its path. Only the fields present in the request body are changed; omitted fields keep their current values. Applies to inboxes, drafts, messages (label changes) and threads (label changes); the response shape mirrors the resource being updated.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the thread to update.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

delete.description: Delete a resource identified by its path (inbox, draft, message, thread, or list entry). Returns an empty success response with no body. For threads, deletion moves the thread to trash unless the `permanent` query flag forces hard deletion.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the thread to delete.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, hard-delete the thread immediately instead of moving it to trash.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: Retrieve a single AgentMail resource by its path identifiers. The exact resource returned depends on the route: an inbox, a draft, a message, a thread, or a single allow/block list entry. The full object (including bodies, attachments and timestamps) is returned, so this is the canonical way to fetch the complete state of one item after listing.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource. Obtain it from create (POST /agentmail/inboxes) or list (GET /agentmail/inboxes).

parameters.name: draft_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the draft to retrieve, as returned when the draft was created.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox. Send as `Bearer <api_key>`; keep it secret and never log it in clear text.

patch.description: Partially update an existing resource identified by its path. Only the fields present in the request body are changed; omitted fields keep their current values. Applies to inboxes, drafts, messages (label changes) and threads (label changes); the response shape mirrors the resource being updated.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource being updated.

parameters.name: draft_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the draft to update.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

delete.description: Delete a resource identified by its path (inbox, draft, message, thread, or list entry). Returns an empty success response with no body. For threads, deletion moves the thread to trash unless the `permanent` query flag forces hard deletion.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource being deleted.

parameters.name: draft_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the draft to delete.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: Retrieve a single AgentMail resource by its path identifiers. The exact resource returned depends on the route: an inbox, a draft, a message, a thread, or a single allow/block list entry. The full object (including bodies, attachments and timestamps) is returned, so this is the canonical way to fetch the complete state of one item after listing.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource. Obtain it from create (POST /agentmail/inboxes) or list (GET /agentmail/inboxes).

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox. Send as `Bearer <api_key>`; keep it secret and never log it in clear text.

patch.description: Partially update an existing resource identified by its path. Only the fields present in the request body are changed; omitted fields keep their current values. Applies to inboxes, drafts, messages (label changes) and threads (label changes); the response shape mirrors the resource being updated.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource being updated.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

requestBody.description: Expects an object; provide at least one of `display_name` or `metadata`.

delete.description: Delete a resource identified by its path (inbox, draft, message, thread, or list entry). Returns an empty success response with no body. For threads, deletion moves the thread to trash unless the `permanent` query flag forces hard deletion.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource being deleted.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: List a collection of resources within an inbox (threads, messages, drafts, events, list entries) or all inboxes. Results are paginated via `limit` + `page_token` and returned newest-first by default. Use the various `include_*` flags to widen the result set to spam/blocked/trash items.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: When listing list entries, restrict to this traffic direction: `send`, `receive`, or `reply`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: When listing list entries, restrict to the allow list or block list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return in one page.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; pass back the `next_page_token` from the previous response to continue.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

post.description: Create a new resource: an inbox, a draft, or an allow/block list entry, depending on the route. The created object is returned in full, including server-assigned identifiers and timestamps.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: When creating a list entry, the traffic direction it should govern: `send`, `receive`, or `reply`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: When creating a list entry, whether it goes on the allow list or block list.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

post.description: Send a reply to all participants of a message (original sender plus all To/Cc recipients), quoting and threading onto the original. Recipients are derived automatically from the source message, so you only supply the new body, optional attachments and headers.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox sending the reply-all.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the message being replied to; its recipients become the reply-all audience.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

post.description: Send an email message. Two routes share this operation: composing and sending a brand-new message from the inbox, or sending a previously saved draft by its `draft_id`. The response returns the resulting message and thread identifiers.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox sending the message.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: Retrieve a single AgentMail resource by its path identifiers. The exact resource returned depends on the route: an inbox, a draft, a message, a thread, or a single allow/block list entry. The full object (including bodies, attachments and timestamps) is returned, so this is the canonical way to fetch the complete state of one item after listing.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource. Obtain it from create (POST /agentmail/inboxes) or list (GET /agentmail/inboxes).

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the thread to retrieve. Returned by the threads list and present on every message.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox. Send as `Bearer <api_key>`; keep it secret and never log it in clear text.

patch.description: Partially update an existing resource identified by its path. Only the fields present in the request body are changed; omitted fields keep their current values. delete.description: Delete a resource identified by its path (inbox, draft, message, thread, or list entry). Returns an empty success response with no body. For threads, deletion moves the thread to trash unless the `permanent` query flag forces hard deletion.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the resource being deleted.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the thread to delete.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, hard-delete the thread immediately instead of moving it to trash.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: List a collection of resources within an inbox (threads, messages, drafts, events, list entries) or all inboxes. Results are paginated via `limit` + `page_token` and returned newest-first by default. Use the various `include_*` flags to widen the result set to spam/blocked/trash items.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return in one page.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; pass back the `next_page_token` from the previous response to continue.

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: Restrict results to items carrying all of the given labels.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Return only items dated before this time, for time-window filtering.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return only items dated after this time, for time-window filtering.

parameters.name: ascending
parameters.description: When true, return results oldest-first instead of the default newest-first.

parameters.name: include_spam
parameters.description: When true, include items classified as spam in the results.

parameters.name: include_blocked
parameters.description: When true, include items from blocked senders in the results.

parameters.name: include_unauthenticated
parameters.description: When true, include items that failed sender authentication (e.g. SPF/DKIM/DMARC).

parameters.name: include_trash
parameters.description: When true, include items currently in trash.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: List a collection of resources within an inbox (threads, messages, drafts, events, list entries) or all inboxes. get.description: Query aggregated activity metrics for an inbox over a time window, optionally bucketed by period and filtered by event type. Useful for dashboards and monitoring inbox throughput.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description: Restrict the metrics to these event types only.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time window to aggregate over.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time window to aggregate over.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Bucket size used to group metrics within the window (e.g. by day or hour).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of metric buckets to return.

parameters.name: descending
parameters.description: When true, return buckets newest-first.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; pass back the `next_page_token` from the previous response to continue.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

post.description: Create a new resource: an inbox, a draft, or an allow/block list entry, depending on the route. The created object is returned in full, including server-assigned identifiers and timestamps.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox under which the resource is created (drafts and list entries). Omit on the route that creates a top-level inbox.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: When creating a list entry, the traffic direction it should govern: `send`, `receive`, or `reply`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: When creating a list entry, whether it goes on the allow list or block list.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.

get.description: Get a single attachment's metadata and a download URL. The attachment can be addressed through its parent message, thread, or draft; all three routes return the same attachment payload.

parameters.name: inbox_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the inbox that owns the attachment.

parameters.name: draft_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the draft the attachment belongs to, when addressing it via a draft.

parameters.name: attachment_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the attachment to fetch, as found on the parent message/thread/draft.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token granting access to the inbox; send as `Bearer <api_key>` and never log it in clear text.
